rulings.Case Title: JESUS THOMAS SOTO VS. PETER LOUIE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Correct Judgment, Alternatively, To Void Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday Aug 9, 2018 line 1. DEFENDANT PETER LOUIE, VIVIENNE LOUIE MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Correct Judgment, Alternatively, To Void Judgment DENIED. Challenge to the judgment had previously been made by the Defendant and rejected by the Court on the motion for reconsideration. CCP 473(d) and case law pertaining to void judgments do not provide a basis to vacate the judgment in this matter. The Court acted within its jurisdiction in interpreting and entering a judgment pursuant to the default provision of the Settlement Agreement (paragraph 25), giving full force and effect to each word of paragraph 25, which referenced paragraphs 14-15 and included additional terms. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BORREGAS COURT ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA GENERAL VS. BOSTON FINANCIAL INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT A ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday Aug 9, 2018 line 2. DEFENDANT BFIM-CALIFORNIA ACQUISITION CORP., ERRONEOUSLY, EDISON CAPITAL HOUSING INVESTMENTS, EDISON CAPITAL HOUSING PARTNERS, XI, LLC DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith as follows: 1. Allege facts showing that each defendant is a proper party to the cause of action for Specific Performance of the Option Agreement; 2. Allege facts showing that the Defendants' acts and/or omissions prevented Plaintiff from exercising its option, i.e. that the timely exercise of the option was impossible in light of Defendant's acts and omissions. 3. Allege facts showing what the deadline for the option was. If Plaintiff pleads in the alternative, each element for each alternative to be clearly alleged: (1) January 1, 2015 and that option was properly exercised prior to the deadline and/or Plaintiff was prevented from exercising by Defendants' acts or omissions; and/or (2) January 1, 2016 (include facts addressing Statute of Frauds) 2015 and that option was properly exercised prior to the deadline and/or Plaintiff was prevented from exercising by Defendant's acts or omissions. 4. Allege facts (not conclusions) that the transfer of the limited partner's interest was improper in light of judicially noticeable documents and Delaware Law. 5. Allege facts that the Plaintiff was entitled to a management fee (in light of the expiration of the Management Agreement) for 2015 and 2016. 6. Re cause of action for declaratory relief, allege a controversy for which declaration is being sought that is not already being addressed in other causes of action. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CARROLL AVENUE SENIOR HOMES, L.P. VS. GREGORY MILLS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, Motion, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration In Support Of Motion To Vacate Default And Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday Aug 9, 2018 line 5. DEFENDANT GREGORY MILLS Notice Of Motion, Motion, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration In Support Of Motion To Vacate Default And Judgment GRANTED, subject to (1) opposition; (2) Defendant posting $1,042 with the Court no later than August 13, 2018 by 4:00 p.m.; and (3) thereafter filing an answer no later than August 13, 2018 by 4:00 p.m. = (501/REQ)
